perturbation.original_text: "Alliance Review Committee" has the meaning given in Section 6.1.
perturbation.changed_text: "Alliance Review Committee" has the meaning given in Section 6.2.
perturbation.explanation: The definition of 'Alliance Review Committee' is changed from Section 6.1 to Section 6.2. This creates an in-text contradiction because the actual formation and responsibilities of the Alliance Review Committee are detailed in Section 6.1, and references to it elsewhere in the document now point to a non-existent or empty section, creating confusion regarding its purpose and function.
perturbation.contradicted_text: As soon as reasonably practicable following the Effective Date, SMBC/Nikko and Moelis Holdings shall form a six person committee comprised of two senior executives from each of SMBC, Nikko and Moelis Holdings to review and discuss the progress of the Strategic Alliance (the "Alliance Review Committee"). Each party shall notify the other parties if it replaces either of its designees to the Alliance Review Committee. The Alliance Review Committee will meet annually and will have the authority to recommend changes to the Strategic Alliance or this Agreement if deemed appropriate. For the avoidance of doubt, the recommendations of the Alliance Review Committee will not be binding upon any party unless and until this Agreement is amended or modified pursuant to Section 15.3.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters the section defining the 'Alliance Review Committee' from 6.1 to 6.2. The contradicted text explicitly states the formation and responsibilities of this committee, referencing the section that now, due to the change, no longer contains that information. This creates a direct conflict, as the committee's description is now in a different location than where it is referenced, leading to ambiguity and uncertainty about its function, as the reference now points to potentially empty or irrelevant content. This is a strong contradiction because it affects the enforceability and understanding of the agreement related to the Alliance Review Committee.

perturbation.original_text: 12.4 Certain Transfer Matters. (a) During the term of the Strategic Alliance, if KM sells all or a portion of KM's Partnership Interests, the SMBC Unit-Holders may sell a pro rata portion of the SMBC Units without regard to any timing or transfer restrictions imposed by this Agreement or the Moelis Holdings Agreement, except that such sale or transfer may not be to a Moelis Competitor.
perturbation.changed_text: 12.4 Certain Transfer Matters. (a) During the term of the Strategic Alliance, if KM sells all or a portion of KM's Partnership Interests, the SMBC Unit-Holders may sell a pro rata portion of the SMBC Units without regard to any timing or transfer restrictions imposed by this Agreement or the Moelis Holdings Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The phrase 'except that such sale or transfer may not be to a Moelis Competitor' is removed, potentially allowing SMBC Unit-Holders to sell their shares to a competitor of Moelis, directly conflicting with the intent of the non-compete clauses and strategic alignment envisioned in the overall agreement. This creates an in-text contradiction because the general spirit and certain explicit sections aim to prevent competitive harm, while this omission allows a direct transfer of ownership to a competitor.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Subject to pre-existing arrangements of either party, each party agrees not to circumvent this Agreement and to act in good faith in the spirit of the Strategic Alliance. Each party shall ensure that its controlled affiliates comply with the terms and conditions of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text prohibited the sale or transfer of SMBC Units to a Moelis Competitor. The changed text removes this restriction. This directly contradicts the spirit of the Strategic Alliance, as stated in the 'Contradicted Text': 'each party agrees not to circumvent this Agreement and to act in good faith in the spirit of the Strategic Alliance.' Removing the restriction allows for a sale that could harm Moelis and undermines the Strategic Alliance, therefore creating a strong in-text contradiction under the Omissions - In Text Contradiction definition.

perturbation.original_text: Notwithstanding anything to the contrary in this Agreement or the Moelis Holdings Agreement, none of SMBC, Moelis Holdings or any of their controlled affiliates shall take any action, including with respect to the acquisition of any Partnership Interests pursuant to the exercise of any right under this Agreement or the Moelis Holdings Agreement, that would reasonably be expected to (i) cause SMBC, or any of its affiliates, to require approval of the Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System under Section 4 of the U.S. Bank Holding Company Act of 1956, as amended (including Regulation Y promulgated thereunder), or otherwise, to acquire or retain any interest in Moelis Holdings or (ii) cause Moelis Holdings to be deemed to be or presumed to be "controlled" by SMBC, or any of its affiliates, for purposes of the U.S. Bank Holding Company Act of 1956, as amended (including Regulation Y promulgated thereunder).
perturbation.changed_text: Notwithstanding anything to the contrary in this Agreement or the Moelis Holdings Agreement, none of SMBC, Moelis Holdings or any of their controlled affiliates shall take any action, including with respect to the acquisition of any Partnership Interests pursuant to the exercise of any right under this Agreement or the Moelis Holdings Agreement, that would reasonably be expected to (i) cause SMBC, or any of its affiliates, to require approval of the Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System under Section 4 of the U.S. Bank Holding Company Act of 1956, as amended (including Regulation Y promulgated thereunder), or otherwise, to acquire or retain any interest in Moelis Holdings.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting clause (ii) of Section 12.6, the contract no longer explicitly prevents actions that would cause Moelis Holdings to be 'controlled' by SMBC under the U.S. Bank Holding Company Act. This creates a significant legal contradiction. The agreement aims for a strategic alliance that does not trigger regulatory control issues. Removing the explicit prohibition against actions leading to SMBC's control over Moelis Holdings creates ambiguity and potential violation of the intent of the parties, particularly concerning regulatory compliance.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each of the parties shall execute and perform this Agreement in compliance with all applicable laws or regulations. Notwithstanding the foregoing, a party shall not be obligated to take any action that violates, infringes or conflicts with or prohibited from taking any action required to be in compliance with any applicable laws or regulations.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly prevented actions that would cause Moelis Holdings to be deemed 'controlled' by SMBC under the U.S. Bank Holding Company Act. The changed text omits this prohibition. The contradicted text states that parties must comply with applicable laws and regulations. By removing the explicit prohibition against actions leading to SMBC's control, the changed text allows for actions that could potentially violate regulatory compliance, creating a direct conflict with the requirement to comply with all applicable laws and regulations, hence a contradiction.
